exercises.name: Oblique Mountain Climbers
exercises.description: In a plank position, bring your knees toward the opposite elbows, engaging the obliques with each movement.

exercises.name: Overhead Forward Sled Drag
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll want to ensure that your arms are locked out over top of your head, biceps to ears.

2) You’ll also want to slightly shorten the distance between the bottom of your ribs and top of your hips to keep your abdominals on and active.

exercises.name: P&C adductor pelvic floor bridges
exercises.description: This exercise is activating the pelvic floor muscles by squeezing the yoga block between the knees as you pull in the pelvic girdle (transverse). Start by squeezing the block and pulling your shoulder blade back and under. Pull your core muscle in nice a deep while you kegel. Slowly light up the hips into a bridge, keep neck and shoulder blades pressed into the floor. Lower and repeat. Take your time and really focus on squeezing the block and maintaining the kegel. It’s a lot to think about, so don’t get frustrated. Your core will improve quickly. Most people struggle at first to pull in and vacuum the abs.

exercises.name: P&C lat stretch
exercises.description: This stretch can be done on a couch or chair. Push hips back and allow roof for your face and spine to stay in line. Hold for about 30 seconds and then bring your hands together and back. Hold for 20-30 seconds.

exercises.name: P&C oblique arch
exercises.description: In this exercise we will be working the transverses, oblique, and the serratus anterior. Make sure the supporting arm is 90 degrees and you’re keeping the shoulder pulled down and back. No shrugging. I want you to pull your core in, kegel and try to pull yourself into a “rainbow”. Each time will improve. If you lose control of your tummy “vacuum “ that’s okay. Take a few deep breathes, reset and continue. Do not rush.

exercises.name: P&C SA isolated row/ scap pull
exercises.description:
This exercise can be done with a cable or with a secured band. Adjust yourself far enough away that when your arm is full extended there is still some tension on the band or cable. Posture is upright and you can adjust your seat (booty) if needed. This tempo is slow about a 3:3 second count. 3 second to pull back, pulling your shoulders down away from the ears and squeezing your shoulder blades together. Release slowly with a count of 3, while
Maintaining good posture.

exercises.name: P&C sacrum supported transverses pulls
exercises.description: For this exercise you will need to support you sacrum (this very low back into top of butt crack) with the yoga block. I prefer the block to be on its taller side, but you can use the shorter side if this is more comfortable. Completely relax and make sure your pelvis is supported comfortably. Legs are 90 degrees. Shoulders are pull back and down. Start by taking some deep breathes pulling your stomach muscles it. The transverse contracts from bottom (by pelvis ) to top (ribs). Pull in and hold for a count of 5 and release. At the end of your reps slowly allow for your legs to fall from side to side. This is a great hip opener at the end.

exercises.name: P&C serratus anterior roll front view
exercises.description: We are rolling out the serratus anterior muscle. When this muscle it accentuates anterior flexion of the arm. (Rolls shoulder forward) and inhibits overhead range of motion and reduces overall shoulder stability.

exercises.name: P&C serratus anterior roll posterior view
exercises.description:
Tips ** make sure the foam roller is right where the bra line would be. Yes this can be painful. Move slowly and range of motion will improve as we progress. 
We are rolling out the serratus anterior muscle. When this muscle it accentuates anterior flexion of the arm. (Rolls shoulder forward) and inhibits overhead range of motion and reduces overall shoulder stability.

exercises.name: P&C table lifts adductor squeeze
exercises.description: Get into a table top position keeping hips over knees and shoulders pulled down away from ears. Squeeze the yoga block between your needs while doing a kegel hold. Pull core in nice a right (breath will be shallow) Slowly lift knees off the ground and return.

exercises.name: Pallof Press
exercises.description:
1.For this exercise, set the rollers at shoulder height. 

2.You’re gonna be focusing on your abdominals even though your arms will be moving. 

3.Have a nice soft knee bend and have a quiet body or a still body where you’re not moving.

4.With your side to the cable, grab the handle with both hands. 

5.You should be approximately arm’s length away from the pulley, with the tension of the weight on the cable.         

5.Align outstretched arm with cable. Pull the cable into your chest. Then, press the cable away. You core should be tight and engaged.

6. Repeat.

exercises.name: Parallel Grip Lat Pulldown
exercises.description:
1.Lean back and grasp the handle using a closed grip with your arms extended straight overhead.

2.Pull the handle down in front to the top of your chest.

3.Straighten your arms completely, returning the handle to the starting position.

4.Remain upright throughout and do not sway back and forth.

exercises.name: Parallel Grip Pull Up
exercises.description:
1.Grab a pull up bar in an upward fist position. Arms and legs straightened making your body hang.

2.Pull your body upwards until your chin is just over the bar.

3.Lower your body returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Pass Through
exercises.description:
1.Stand upright holding a PVC pipe or wooden dowel in a wide grip.

2.Raise arms, passing the PVC pipe overhead, rotate the shoulder until the pipe touches lower back. The wider the grip, the easier it is. The narrower the grip, the more intense the stretch.

3.Reverse the movement to return to starting position. 

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Pelvic floor wall sit w/ db curl press
PFM DB alt lower leg lifts
exercises.description: Inhale and exhale, tuck pelvis in and flatten back to floor. Squeeze core tight and slightly lower Db behind head. Bring legs up to bent position and alternate lowering one at a time. Perform 8-10 and break, breathe and reset.

exercises.name: PFM hip hop w/ glute bridge adduction
exercises.description: Squeeze ball or towel between knees. Place hands under butt, breathe in and out to set your PFM and pull them into the floor. Lift glutes slighting off ground like a hop, place feet down and glute raise into a bridge. Keep core tight and squeeze the ball or towel tightly.

exercises.name: PFM kick tucks
exercises.description: Inhale abs exhale to active PFM , exhale and squeeze core, lift into table top position and kick one leg back and tuck your knee in and squeeze. Do 5 per side and reset with breathing. It is important to take breaks and not rush through these.

exercises.name: PFM progression table top knee taps
PFM table top block raises w/adduction
exercises.description: I want you to go into the rails top. Use a mirror to make sure your legs are 90 degrees and back is flat. Squeeze the block between knees and slowly lift knees up to hover. Hold 2-3 sec and come back down. I adjust my shirt here for you to see that my core is nice and tight and pulled in. This will make breathes shallow. Focus on pulling your stomach in. You can break to take some breathes and reset.

exercises.name: PFM table top knee taps
exercises.description: Deep breath in and out 2-3 times. Pull core tightly in, (this will make big breathes difficult) go into a table top position and tap the opposite knee with hand. Perform 5 per side. Come down and deep breath 2-3 times ... repeat

exercises.name: PFM wall activation
exercises.description: You will want shoes or bare feet so you do not slip. This is a pelvic floor activation movement. Keep your core tight and engaged. Push through your hands as if you are pushing the floor away from you. Hold for 5 sec and then individually tap each foot down. Hold again 5 sec and repeat. Reps will be considered 5 sec hold, tap / tap =1

exercises.name: Pike Push Up to Reach
exercises.alternateName: shoulder push up to toe touch
exercises.description:
Start in a pike position with hands and feet on the floor, hips raised toward the ceiling, forming an inverted “V.” 

Perform a push-up by lowering your head toward the floor and then pressing back up to the pike position. 

After the push-up, alternate reaching each hand toward the opposite foot before returning to the starting pike position.

exercises.name: Piked Push Up
exercises.description:
1.Get into push up position, palms flat on the floor and slightly more than shoulder-width apart, your feet should be together and your legs straight. 

2.Walk your feet towards your hand, lifting your butt into the air.

3.Press up through your shoulders, hold then return to starting position.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Pilates - (Bent Knee) Side-Lying Leg Lift
exercises.description: Lie on one side with knees bent. Lift the top leg while keeping the knee bent, engaging your glutes. Lower with control and repeat.

exercises.name: Pilates - Advanced Side Plank
exercises.description: Start in a side plank with one forearm on the mat. Stack your feet and lift your top arm toward the ceiling. Hold for balance or add hip dips for a challenge.

exercises.name: Pilates - Arm Reach with Ball under Ribs
exercises.description: Lie on your side with a ball under your ribs. Reach your arm overhead to stretch the side body.

exercises.name: Pilates - Ball Crunch
exercises.description: Lie on your back with knees bent and hold a ball at your chest. Perform a crunch while keeping the ball stable.

exercises.name: Pilates - Ball Pass
exercises.alternateName: Core Ball Pass Hands to Feet
exercises.description: Lie on your back and hold a ball between your feet. Pass the ball to your hands as you crunch up, then extend back down.

exercises.name: Pilates - Bear Plank Adductor Squeeze
exercises.description: Start in a bear plank position (knees hovering above the mat). Place a ball or block between your knees and squeeze while holding the plank.

exercises.name: Pilates - Bicycle
exercises.description: Lie on your back, lift legs, alternate extending legs in a pedaling motion while twisting your torso to engage obliques.

exercises.name: Pilates - Control Balance
exercises.description: Lie on your back, lift legs overhead into a rollover position, extend one leg up while keeping the other in place, switch sides with control.

exercises.name: Pilates - Corkscrew
exercises.description: Lie on your back, lift legs to 90 degrees, engage the core, and draw controlled circular motions with both legs together.

exercises.name: Pilates - Donkey Kick to Cross Knee Tap
exercises.alternateName: Pilates - Ball Glute Kick with cross over
exercises.description: Start on hands and knees. Perform a donkey kick, then bring the knee across to tap the opposite elbow.
